February 21, 1985 
Melvyn W. Price 
Supervising Deputy County Counsel 
County of Sacramento 
700 H Street, Suite 2650 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Price: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our Advice No. A-85-0l2 
This letter is in response to your request for advice 
regarding the 1984 amendments to Government Code Section 
843081/ and the effect on the decisions of the planning 
commission. 
FACTS 
In a previous advice letter concerning Section 84308 (No. 
A-83-080), we concluded that a planning commission makes a 
decision within the meaning of Section 84308 when: 
1. 
Acting in its advisory capacity, the planning 
commission votes on a recommendation to the Board of 
Supervisors1 and 
2. 
Acting in its decisionmaking capacity, the planning 
commission votes on a matter. 
Among other changes to Section 84308, the word "final" was 
inserted before the word "decision." 
See Section 84308(b) and 
(d) • 
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise specified. 
The amendments are in Stats. 1984, 
Ch. 1681. 


Melvyn W. Price 
February 21, 1985 
Page 2 
QUESTION 
Does the term "final decision" in Section 84308 as amended 
change the conclusions reached in our previous advice letter? 
DISCUSSION 
The insertion of the word "final" in Section 84308 does not 
change the meaning or application of the word "decision" from 
that given to it previously in our advice letter; it was 
intended to clarify the calculation of the time limits in the 
statute. 
Accordingly, when a planning commission votes on a 
recommendation or on another matter, it has made a "final 
decision" within the meaning of Section 84308 even if there will 
be a subsequent decision in the same matter made by the Board of 
Supervisors. 
If I may be of any further assistance, or if you have any 
additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at 
(916) 322-5901. 
DMF:plh 
Sincerely, 
~ 
~~hburn 
/~~---
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division 


L. B. Elam 
County Counsel 
Fred G. Williams 
Assistant County Counsel 
COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO

Dear Mr. Price:

January 29, 1985 
Re: 
A-85-012 
Enforcement 
322-6""1 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6""" 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Maura Fishburn, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
BAM:nwm 
VjfY truly yours, 
!JWYLULti ljtW:;/U1G-
. Barbara A. Milman 
General Counsel
